item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much. I really do like this one, even if it's basically the black sheep of my mainline scips. Appreciate your help with the image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Noting I made a CC compliant version of the image for this skip that has now replaced the old image.

Noticed that trenner already covered that in their beginning comment, whoops!

This skip is still very near and dear to my heart. I still am a huge fan of it even after all these years.

item.title: Re: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: This image contains only the eyes and mouths from the original photo. There is no reason to remove it.

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image was not released under an explicit Creative Commons license by the original website owner and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is going in my favorites, it actually is real scary to think of a face growing on yours. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's been awhile since I wrote this, but I believe this is 100% correct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you to my true crime podcast obsession for helping me with this one

The names are:
Still can't figure out colours lmao
  • Thomas Dillon (the shooty one) = I'm really not sure on this one, but it seemed the most accurate?
  • James Swann (big shotgun) = the 'Shotgun Stalker'
  • Albert Fish (mail sometimes) = rapist and cannibal, sent a letter to the mother of one of his victims
  • Gordon Northcott (sexual crimes) = the Wineville chicken coop murders (not sure about this one)
  • Charles Ng (military buff) = ex-Marine, crimes with Leonard Lakes
  • Henry Lee Lucas (lies and bluffs) =
  • Ed Gein (skin to wear) = the Butcher of Plainfield
  • Harvey Glatman (sado-masochistic flair) = the Lonely Hearts Killer

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Super cool. Serial killer liches as stickers. And Edward is still missing…?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Helluva lot more chilling than I'd initially thought. When that realization hit me…shudder…fridge-horror gold.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I'm okay with this change.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Stopped. Edits made.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ show block
– hide block

Hmm. I was having a bit of trouble on this one, but I found out about SCP-248. Seeing as they both have effects when applied in sticker form, I think that they might have a common origin. Don't want to directly link it to the factory though, so I'll go this route.

I propose the following addition after Dr. Carver's note.

Addendum: Due to the similar method of application between SCP-1439 and SCP-248, it is currently theorized that they share a common origin. However, SCP-1439 does not carry any mention of "The Factory" and doesn't appear to be mass-produced, so it is currently theorized that SCP-1439 was discontinued before many instances were created. The credibility of this theory is unknown at this time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The thing that creeps me out the most is that the stickers have the personality of several serial killers.

Like it's scary enough to have something trying to kill you, but Albert Fish trying to kill you?

Jesus Christ please don't make me think about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: According to word of god upthread, the Thomas the Tank Engine connection is a red herring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but I don't understand the Thomas The Tank Engine connection since I don't see the facial distortion in this page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: me too thanks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it , upvote

item.title: Re: Just an Idea
item.content:encoded: I highly doubt an article like that would survive the site.

item.title: Just an Idea
item.content:encoded: I like the concept but as odd as it might sound I wish they were so other number. I somehow was hoping SCP 1439 would end up being D-9341 from containment breach.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh god I lost my shit
– hide block

They’re 2, they’re 4, they’re 6, they’re 8
They’re filled with rage and lust and hate
They know what they want to do
They want to rape and murder you
So lock your doors at night and day
And hope that they stay far away
Their own psychosis never ends
Thomas and his friends

Thomas, he’s the shooty one
James sure loves his big shotgun
Albert sends you mail sometimes
Gordon loves sexual crimes
Charles, the military buff
Henry kills and lies and bluffs
Edward wants some skin to wear
Harvey, sado-masochistic flair!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this but reading the the test logs I was thinking damn the foundation test methodology sucks. Stick random stickers to random items. Come on guys only change one variable at a time remember :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see the end of the article. I see tags. I see a HUGE chunk of whitespace with no discernible purpose. Then I highlight it. Copy it. Paste it. VERY nice. But kind of hard to miss when you notice the formatting of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're welcome. Not to go on a tangent here, but the Sodor Mythos has become a "hip thing on the Internet" because of the history behind it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: albert reminded me of the thing, great stuff :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ha! That's awesome, and good to know, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, there was an Albert in the books the show is based on. He was a friend of Edward's from Edward's pre-Sodor days.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly didn't think anyone would ever see that. I wrote it up for fun while at work, and figured there was no harm in hiding it in the article for my own amusement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see you've added a nice little thing at the bottom of the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was a very charismatic hamster, to be fair. They were just caught off guard for a moment, before taking control of the situation and incinerating the problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't help but be baffled that the Foundation managed to lose control of a hamster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the end of the article better, but it wasn't until I read Felgraf's spoiler below that I could even begin to hazard a guess as to what's behind that. It still bothers me too much to change my downvote - if just some details as to what's actually hiding behind that expungement were released, enough to make it possible to hazard a guess in article, I'd probably change to an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That is certainly what I am going to tell everyone.
It's so wonderful/horrible that I don't care if it's true or not, I'll spread it around until it becomes true by consensus belief.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're probably right about that, and I might be able to rework that section. Just goes to show that I really am more of an idea person than a writer. It's frustrating when I can't find the right words or sentence structure to show people what I'm envisioning in my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you're right about that final note; now that I've made so many changes to it and the last experiment it really doesn't belong there. I'll take it out of the collapsible tomorrow as I'm on my xbox right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cripes this is creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The test logs really make this, but I couldn't figure out what was going on until I read them. I think some reworking of the description is in order.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel a bit of pride tha ti managed to get the serial killer part before i hit the comments. It was the Albert experiment that convinced me.
There are a few spots that feel a bit off, the most notable one being the part about Testing being suspended in the containment proceedures. I kind of feel like that should be pulled into an addendum and rephrased. I really don't mind the last expungement, though there could perhaps be some more tweaks to it.
Overall well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These are some killer stickers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You missed a SCP-XXXX in the Experiment Log.

Initial conclusion is that close proximity to a human brain greatly changes the way SCP-XXXX-1 manifests.

Great article though, I enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to believe it was an unlucky coincidence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not a fan of the concept, but it's written okay, and I had some hope for the hook… but dat expungement. Y. You don't even throw us a hint at what we should be thinking there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It truly is a chicken and egg situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, wait, wait, wait, wait…

+ SPOILERS
- SPOILERS

Did Thomas the Tank Engine get its names from serial killers????????!!!!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can respect that. I just don't think that fully explaining what's behind the expungement is a good idea. I can imagine so many different ways it could manifest, and what it would look like, and I feel like giving a description of what happens in that last test would ruin it. That's why I edited the doctor's final note. I may have to revisit it and try to give more context…will continue to think on it.

Edit: I realized that regardless of my intentions, and how it all works in my head, it still just looks like cliche, lazy expungement. I've done some more editing to try and improve that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That almost lost my vote, too, but the fact that I could already tell basically what happened, and just had to imagine it made it passable for me. Plus I think the SCP deserves to live just over-all.

Eh, guess we'll just disagree on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was going to upvote this until the vad expungement, which just left a bad taste in my mouth after reading a p. decent article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's only the red herring though, he said.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You got it, kudos!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AHA. I think I've got it.

+ SPOILERS
– hide block

They're the first names of Serial Killers, aren't they?
Charles Ng (who worked with a Leo)
Harvey Glatman (serial killer and rapist)
Albert Fish or Albert Desalvo
Gordon Northcott
etc etc

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

YOU

YOU SONUVA

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There was a Charlie though.

EDIT: You got it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty sure there was no Charles. After looking at the names used for each test… I like this even more.

Edit: what was it, Albert then? Gah, I'm getting old.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Somebody finally got the red herring, hooray! 7 of the 8 names are from Thomas…but one is not. However, all 8 names are related to each other in a different way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The names are significant in that 7 of the 8 are related to each other in one way. But all 8 names are related to each other in a completely different way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edward, Thomas, Gordon, Henry, Albert, James, Charles, and Harvey. The first three stickers are light blue, while the others are green, grey, red, purple, and brown, respectively

Thomas the Tank Engine. You had to use the names from that damn show. And then you had to make the anomalous effect fucking creepy.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just realized that there is a deeper meaning to this beyond "Stickers that turn things into silly cartoons." Now I'm trying to figure out what that is based on the names.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to neutral vote due to being well written but uninteresting, but then you put the image of an orgasming lamp into my head. Take my upvote, godammit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. I like yours much better and I am going to steal it if you don't mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I tried to add some context to that with an edit to the Dr.'s final note. I may consider adding it to the experiment's notes instead. I stand by my expungement still, as I think it can work with the right clues surrounding it, but I could definitely change my mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it overall, but downvoted because of the last expungement. You'd been doing great about detail up until then, the sudden 'a whole experiment is deleted…but it's aweful!!" vibe really killed it for me.
